name: HYPOPLASTIC LEFT HEART SYNDROME
definition: SHONE SYNDROME = AORTIC ATRESIA underdevelopment of left side of heart characterized by
discriminating_features: parachute mitral valve = all chordae tendineae arise from single fused papillary muscle

general: parachute mitral valve = all chordae tendineae arise from single fused papillary muscle
CXR: interstitial + alveolar pulmonary edema ← pulmonary venous hypertension with severely
normal pulmonary vasculature (with wide nonrestrictive interatrial communication in 20%)
small left ventricular cavity (apex of LV and RV should be at same level)
diastolic flow reversal in narrow ascending aorta is DIAGNOSTIC
herniation + prolapse of foramen ovale flap into RA
hypoplastic ascending aorta (< 5 mm = aortic atresia)
absent / grossly distorted mitral valve echoes
ANGIO: retrograde flow in ascending aorta + aortic arch + coronary arteries via PDA
stringlike ascending aorta < 6 mm in diameter
